title: ranunculus paludosus var. cinerascens
intro: Ranunculus paludosus var. cinerascens, also known as marsh buttercup, is a plant species within the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Ranunculus paludosus var. cinerascens include its potential to improve digestion, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although the preparation methods and dosages may vary depending on the intended use.
